page: Microsoft Azure L16s_v2 is an x86_64 server instance belonging to the LSv2 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7551 processor running at 3.2 GHz, with 8 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 4004 GB of local storage, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between storage capacity and raw processing power. The AMD EPYC 7551 CPU exhibits poor single-core performance in stress-ng tests (bottom 10%) and weak multi-core performance (bottom 25%). Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression are weak. However, the instance maintains average performance (middle 50%) in multi-core Geekbench tests, software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. Memory bandwidth is average for read operations but drops to weak for large-block write operations.

Given the massive 4004 GB of local storage and average performance in database operations, Redis SET operations (9.53 million operations/sec), and static web serving, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy workloads that do not require intense CPU calculations. It is well-suited for database hosting, large-scale caching, and static web serving. It is poorly suited for CPU-bound tasks, text processing, or large language model inference, where it scores in the bottom 10% to 25%.
description: Microsoft Azure L16s_v2 is an x86_64 virtualized server from the LSv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7551 processor clocked at 3.2 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 4004 GB of local storage. While the instance offers high storage capacity and average memory read bandwidth, its CPU performance is a notable tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 10% for single-core stress tests and the bottom 25% for multi-core processing. Benchmark results show average performance for database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving, but poor results for text processing and LLM inference. This configuration is best suited for storage-intensive applications, database hosting, and caching workloads where massive local storage is required, rather than compute-heavy or high-throughput write tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L16s_v2 is an LSv2-family server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 4004 GB local storage, offering a high-capacity storage profile with moderate CPU performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L16s_v2 features 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 4004 GB local storage, optimized for storage-heavy database workloads.
tagline: A storage-dense virtual instance combining 128 GB of memory with 4004 GB of local capacity for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7551 processor at 3.2 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
4004 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average performance in database operations and Redis SET tasks
Weak single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark results
Best suited for storage-intensive databases and static web serving
